rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION VS. GABRIEL DIAMOND REGUS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Otion To Set Aside Dismissal, Enforce Settlement, And Enter Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Set Aside Dismissal, Enforce Settlement, And Enter Judgment. Granted. The dismissal of the consolidated matter of California State Automobile Association Inter-Insurance Bureau v. Gabriel Diamond Regus (CGC-10-498991) erroneously included the matter of State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company v. Gabriel Regus (CGC-10-499802). The dismissal is set aside as to of State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company v. Gabriel Regus (CGC-10-499802) pursuant to CCP sec. 473(b). Judgment shall be entered against Defendant Gabriel Regus in the amount of $4,140.14 plus interest and costs pursuant to the Stipulation filed on March 22, 2013. Defendant has not established that he complied with the terms of the Stipulation, as there is no evidence that Defendant paid Plaintiff $ 1,000 by January 31, 2013. The Judgment shall be stayed 30 days to give Defendant an opportunity to pay Plaintiff the $ 1,000. If Defendant fails to pay within 30 days, Judgment shall be entered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CARL HILSZ VS VERA CORT, et a
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Answers To Special Interrogatories In Compliance With Court Order, For Issue Sanctions And For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft And Her Attorneys
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CARL HILSZ' Motion To Compel Further Answers To Special Interrogatories In Compliance With Court Order, For Issue Sanctions And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Her Attorneys. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The moving party has not complied with Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall e-mail a copy of the moving papers and proof of service to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com no later than 4 pm today, and provide a copy of them at the hearing on February 11, 2014 by 8:45 a.m. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LONNIE JACKSON VS. SAW ENTERTAINMENT LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion An Motion To Compel Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF LONNIE JACKSON's Motion To Compel Depositions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. Under CCP section 2024.020, the discovery cutoff date was 30 days before the original trial date of December 9, 2013. Even assuming, as both parties do, that the applicable discovery cutoff date was January 31, 2014, the depositions sought to be compelled would occur beyond the discovery cutoff date, regardless of when they were first noticed. CCP section 2024.050 applies to plaintiff's motion, and plaintiff has not established the factors set forth in section 20204.050 so as to warrant the requested discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ASHWIN NANDA DDS VS. WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF ASHWIN NANDA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Denied. Plaintiff fails to produce any on point authority supporting the contention that he is a non-exempt employee. Negri v. Koning & Associates (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 392 addressed the administrative exemption, not the professional exemption as it is applied to dentists. The definition of "salary base" found in 29 C.F.R. § 541.602 do not apply to dentists. (29 C.F.R. § 541.304(d).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ASHWIN NANDA DDS VS. WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Summ Adj; Appendix
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Denied. Defendant Western Dental Services, Inc. has not met its initial burden to demonstrate that Plaintiff Ashwin Nanda claims in the present action are barred by res judicata. Defendant has not demonstrated that the claims or issues raised in the present action are identical to the claims or issues litigated in Nanda v. Western Dental Services (N.D. Cal. Case no. C 12-04654-PJH). (Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788, 797.) In the present action, Plaintiff's claims are based on misclassification as an exempt employee for wage and hour violations. (See Req. Jud. Notice, Exhibits B, C.) In the federal action, Plaintiff's claims are based on violation of Title VII of the Civil Rights Act for employment discrimination. (Req. Jud. Notice, Exhibit H.) These two actions do not involve the same primary right. (Boeken, supra, 48 Cal.4th at 797-98. See also Eichman v. Fotomat Corp. (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 1170, 1174.) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE FINLEY VS. DREXEL A. BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT DREXEL BRADSHAW's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Denied. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") states that "[e]ach defendant named above is a natural person." (FAC, 4.) CCP sec. 412.30 only applies to actions against corporations. Although the language of CRC 3.221(c) states that a Plaintiff must serve a copy of an ADR package with the complaint, Defendant cites no authority that a Court may quash service for violation of a Rule of Court. Plaintiff is ordered to serve Defendant with an ADR packet in compliance with CRC 3.221(c) within 10 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LFG NATIONAL CAPITAL, LLC VS. JOSEPH M. ALIOTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories-First Set
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANTs JOSEPH ALIOTO and THE ALIOTO LAW FIRM, A SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP's Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories-First Set. Motion granted as to Nos. 2, 9, 10, 15 (as modified), 16 (as modified), 26 (as modified), 27, and 28. Within 15 days of service of notice of entry of order, Plaintiff LFG National Capital, LLC shall serve verified further responses. Objections are overruled as to Interrogatories nos. 2, 9, 10, 15, 16, 27, and 28. The Court finds Interrogatories nos. 2, 9, 10, 27, and 28 are sufficiently definite to define the scope of the interrogatory. Interrogatories nos. 15 and 16 are sufficient, but to avoid any possible ambiguity, the interrogatories are modified to reference the Plaintiff as the recipient and to refer to all payments made by Defendants to Plaintiff. For Interrogatories no. 26, the objection is sustained as to the phrase, "anything else," and the Interrogatory is modified to strike that phrase; the balance of the interrogatory is sufficiently intelligible to merit a response by Plaintiff. Monetary sanctions of $ 2,500.00 are awarded in favor of Defendants Joseph M. Alioto and The Alioto Law Firm and against Plaintiff LFG National Capital, LLC to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO et al VS. EVEREST NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al*****TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT EVEREST NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Transfer Action. Granted. The action is transferred to San Bernardino County. The basis that venue is proper in San Francisco is the principal place of business for Defendant C.V. Starr & Co. ("Starr"). (Opp., p. 4:20.) In determining whether venue is proper, the Court may consider evidence. (3 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th ed. 2008) Actions, § 895, p. 1126.) Based on the stipulation of Plaintiffs and Defendant Everest National Insurance Company ("Everest"), Defendant Starr is improperly joined as a defendant in the first, second and third causes of action. Defendant Starr had no role in the denial of the Claim at issue in this action. (CCP § 395(b).) For the fourth cause of action, Defendant Everest has presented evidence that in its role as underwriter, Defendant Starr did not engage in the alleged any advertising or correspondence that Defendant Everest would promptly pay for large losses. (See Gervasio Decl., 12.) Plaintiff has not presented any evidence to the contrary, and relies solely on the allegations of its Second Amended Complaint. (Opp., p. 14:12 - 15:5.) The convenience of witnesses and ends of justice would be promoted by transfer. (CCP § 397(c).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN E. FERRY VS. FAWZI SALEH NIJEM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Reclassify Case
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014, Line 14, PLAINTIFF JOHN FERRY's Motion To Reclassify Case. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FARZANEH AMINI PHD et al VS. RAYMOND JETER PHD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Of Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANT RAYMOND JETER's Motion For Order Of Dismissal. Off calendar as untimely under CCP § 1005(b). The motion was filed and served by mail on January 21, 2014. Moving party only provided 15 days court days notice and failed to account for the 5 calendar day extension for mail service. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)
